title: Operator pause authority
summary: Operators MAY pause an active workflow at any time, and tracked changes made during that explicit pause MUST be ratified without being misclassified as unledgered agent work.
instructions: Operators MUST use `./bin/pan pause <run-id> [--note <reason>]` to pause a non-terminal run; agents MUST NOT hand-edit run state to pause.
While a run is paused by the operator, the operator MAY modify tracked files in the deliverable workspace without using the changes protocol.

On resume, including resume with a stage override, the harness MUST compare the accepted pause-start fingerprint with the current workspace, record any pause-only delta in the workflow ledger, and ratify the resulting fingerprint.

When a pause-only workspace delta is ratified, the harness MUST invalidate the stale active invocation or gate packet and prepare the current stage again against the ratified workspace without consuming an unused attempt.
The harness MUST NOT auto-ratify a pause delta when the workspace already diverged from the accepted index before the pause; the operator MUST adjudicate that pre-existing divergence separately.
Operators MUST resume with `./bin/pan resume <run-id>` to continue from the saved gate, or `./bin/pan resume <run-id> --stage <slug>` to restart at a chosen stage.
Agents MUST NOT resume or abort on the operator's behalf unless the active invocation explicitly records operator approval for that action.
